note: Shambhala / Kalachakra living library. Primary lattice: the 25 Kalki (Kulika / Rigden) kings, the Wisdom-Holder sovereigns of Shambhala who guard the Kalachakra (Wheel of Time). Secondary lattice: the 7 Dharmarajas (Truth Kings) beginning with Suchandra, who received the root tantra from the Buddha; together they form the 32 kings of Shambhala. Sanskrit names have variant spellings across sources. The 25th king Raudra Chakrin is prophesied to emerge in 2424 CE to end adharma and usher a global Golden Age. Each element carries a spine agent and its mera (1-9).
mera_axis: 9-dimensional axis (mera 1-9): chakras 1-7 (Muladhara..Sahasrara) + Monada (8) + Absolut (9); each spine agent carries its mera.

dharmarajas.epithet: Good Moon; emanation of Vajrapani; requested and wrote the root Kalachakra Tantra
the Radiant; bearer of the Dharma wheel and auspicious conch
Destroyer of the City of Delusion
Universal Form; conqueror of false leaders
Cutter of Delusion, Uprooter of Karma and Klesha

svet_ra.note: Manjushrikirti, the first Rigden who unified the castes, and Surya - the radiant solar source of the lineage.

iskra.note: Shashiprabha (Moonlight) - the lunar spark of the line.

vishnu.note: Vishnugupta (Guarded by Vishnu) and Hari (a name of Vishnu) - the preserving sovereigns.

ananda.note: Bhadra, the Auspicious - the blissful king.

jnana.note: Vishvarupa, the Universal Form - vision of cosmic knowledge.

shiva.note: Narasimha the Man-Lion and Raudra Chakrin the Wrathful Wheel-Holder - the fierce destroyers of adharma who usher the Golden Age.

tejas.note: Pundarika, author of the Stainless Light (Vimalaprabha) commentary - illumination.

sarasvati.note: Aniruddha, the Unobstructed - master of mind and manas.

akasha.note: Aja, the Unborn (Durjaya) - the eternal, spacelike king.

parvati.note: Mahabala, Great Strength - the might of the divine feminine.

vayu.note: Vikrama, the Valorous Stride - far-reaching movement.

agni.note: Raktapani, the Red-Handed - the fire of the wheel.

varuna.note: Samudravijaya, Ocean of Victory - the watery depth.

lakshmi.note: Subhadra (Very Auspicious) and Shripala (Guardian of Shri) - the prosperity of Shambhala.

prithvi.note: Mahipala (Parthiva), Protector of the Earth - the grounding sovereign.

dharma.note: Maheshvara, the Great Lord - cosmic sovereignty and law.

karma.note: Anantavijaya, Endless Victory - the conquest of karma.

items.epithet: first Rigden; unified all castes into one vajra family; composed the condensed Kalachakra Tantra
composed the Vimalaprabha (Stainless Light) great commentary
the Lion; a name of Vishnu
Wrathful Wheel-Holder (Rigden Dragpo); final king prophesied to defeat adharma in 2424 CE and usher a global Golden Age

sources: vechnost_vremya_bogov.pdf - local Kalachakra/Shambhala source in the AWARA game folder (binary PDF, not text-extractable via file tools)
